summary: Provision new SQS queues — standard or FIFO. Greenfield today (no SQS queue exists yet in this estate) — the create stands up the queue with its delivery and encryption settings; dead-letter routing and a custom access policy are engineer follow-ups.

operations.title: Provision a new SQS queue
operations.summary: Request a brand-new standard or FIFO queue; an engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: Stand up a queue as a complete specification: standard vs FIFO, retention, visibility timeout, and encryption. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish — nothing is applied without review.

params.name: queue_name
params.label: Queue name
params.help: Up to 80 characters. A FIFO queue's name must end in .fifo (matching the FIFO queue toggle below).

params.name: fifo_queue
params.label: FIFO queue
params.help: FIFO guarantees exact-once, in-order delivery within a message group at lower throughput; standard is unordered/at-least-once and scales further. Cannot be changed after creation.

params.name: content_based_deduplication
params.label: Content-based deduplication
params.help: FIFO only. Deduplicates on a SHA-256 hash of message body instead of a caller-supplied dedup id.

params.name: deduplication_scope
params.label: Deduplication scope
params.help: FIFO only. messageGroup unlocks higher throughput (with fifo_throughput_limit set to perMessageGroupId).

params.name: fifo_throughput_limit
params.label: FIFO throughput limit
params.help: FIFO only. perMessageGroupId requires deduplication_scope=messageGroup and gives each message group its own throughput quota.

params.name: visibility_timeout_seconds
params.label: Visibility timeout (seconds)
params.help: How long a received message is hidden from other consumers before it becomes visible again.

params.name: message_retention_seconds
params.label: Message retention (seconds)
params.help: How long an undelivered message is kept. 1,209,600 seconds = 14 days (the maximum).

params.name: max_message_size
params.label: Max message size (bytes)
params.help: 262,144 bytes (256 KiB) is the AWS maximum.

params.name: delay_seconds
params.label: Delivery delay (seconds)
params.help: Delays every message this many seconds before consumers can see it.

params.name: receive_wait_time_seconds
params.label: Long-poll wait time (seconds)
params.help: Above 0 enables long polling, reducing empty-receive API calls and cost.

params.name: sqs_managed_sse_enabled
params.label: SSE-SQS (AWS-managed encryption)
params.help: Amazon SQS-managed server-side encryption. Mutually exclusive with the customer-managed KMS key below — pick one during review.

params.name: kms_master_key_id
params.label: Customer-managed KMS key (SSE-KMS)
params.help: Leave blank to use SSE-SQS above instead of a customer-managed key.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this queue is for
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this queue.

decisions: Dead-letter queue wiring (redrive_policy) and a custom queue access policy are engineer follow-ups after creation.
